channel.title: SpotPulse Trending
channel.description: Live web pulse — India & Global intelligence dashboard.

item.title: India is building a stealth combat drone as heavy as a fighter jet – Here’s how it may change air warfare in South Asia - Zee News
item.description: India is building a stealth combat drone as heavy as a fighter jet – Here’s how it may change air warfare in South Asia Zee News

item.title: Market 'Yet to Fully Experience' Aluminum Shortfall from Iran, Says Timna Tanners
item.description: Shortfalls could persist longer than current expectations and drive up the price of aluminum as an impact of war with Iran says Timna Tanners, managing director of equity research for Wells Fargo. Tanners talked about the different impacts of the war on commodity prices, including copper which neared record highs on Tuesday due to a rise in demand from China and fears about supplies of sulfur in the Mideast. (Source:

item.title: Hackers Insert Malware Into Mistral AI Software Download - Decrypt
item.description: Hackers Insert Malware Into Mistral AI Software Download Decrypt

item.title: Bowie State University awarded $727,500 grant to expand solar energy and clean energy workforce programs - WMAR 2 News Baltimore
item.description: Bowie State University awarded $727,500 grant to expand solar energy and clean energy workforce programs WMAR 2 News Baltimore

item.title: Monsoon may hit Andamans this week: IMD
item.description: The India Meteorological Department has launched an AI-enabled monsoon forecasting system offering localized weather information up to four weeks in advance for farmers. A pilot service for Uttar Pradesh provides high-resolution rainfall forecasts at 1km resolution for up to 10 days, utilizing advanced AI and observational data.

item.title: Iran: US, Israel launched this 'war of aggression' - Breakingthenews.net
item.description: Iran: US, Israel launched this 'war of aggression' Breakingthenews.net

item.title: Air India: Crisis deepens ahead of final Ahmedabad crash report - BBC
item.description: Air India: Crisis deepens ahead of final Ahmedabad crash report BBC

item.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras to Guide IPO Pricing Above Range
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. is guiding prospective investors that it expects to price its initial public offering above the top of its marketed range, according to people familiar with the matter, as demand soars for the chipmaker’s stock.

item.title: Asian Stocks Under Pressure as US Inflation Rises: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks are poised for a weak open after losses on Wall Street, with the dollar strengthening and bonds falling as inflation data showed the impact of energy disruptions linked to the war in Iran.

item.title: Oil Holds Gain With Iran’s Exports Strained as Conflict Drags On
item.description: Oil was steady as the conflict in the Middle East drags on and keeps the vital Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, with shipments from Iran’s main export terminal appearing to come to a standstill.

item.title: Anthropic in Talks to Buy Developer Tools Startup Used by OpenAI, Google
item.description: Anthropic is in advanced talks to acquire developer tools startup Stainless for at least $300 million, according to a person with knowledge of the deal. The four-year-old Stainless sells software to model makers including Anthropic, OpenAI and Google that helps developers, non-technical people and AI agents more quickly access AI models. Such software has been in particular demand as AI agents—such as Claude Code and

item.title: Foxconn Ransomware Attack Shows Nothing Is Safe Forever
item.description: Famous for helping build Apple’s iPhones, Foxconn just suffered another cyberattack, highlighting the perils of warehousing some of the world’s most valuable data.

item.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
item.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening t

item.title: Inflation Accelerates as Iran War Drives Up Energy Costs - The New York Times
item.description: Inflation Accelerates as Iran War Drives Up Energy Costs The New York Times

item.title: Mexico Rating Outlook Revised to Negative at S&P as Debts Mount
item.description: S&P Global Ratings revised Mexico’s credit outlook to negative from stable, citing persistently weak fiscal results, rising debt levels and weak economic growth.

item.title: ‘Mini Shai-Hulud’ malware compromises hundreds of open-source packages in sprawling supply-chain attack - CyberScoop
item.description: ‘Mini Shai-Hulud’ malware compromises hundreds of open-source packages in sprawling supply-chain attack CyberScoop

item.title: Ever since Elon Musk took over, banning social media accounts became common: Iltija Mufti
item.description: She said banning the social media accounts has impacted her ability “to raise issues of public importance and reach out to people over misgovernance”

item.title: ‘I don’t think about Americans’ financial situation’: Trump says stopping Iran nuclear program is ‘all’ that matters
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday said that he is not concerned regarding the economic impact of the US-Iran war on Americans, adding that the only thing that matters is that Tehran cannot have a nuclear weapon.

item.title: Insurance pool of Rs 13,000 crore for Indian ships
item.description: The Indian government has launched a $1.5 billion Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool, backed by a $1.4 billion sovereign guarantee, to offer risk cover for Indian vessels on international routes, including war zones. This initiative aims to reduce shipping insurance premiums and guarantee coverage even when global insurers withdraw due to rising risks.

item.title: AICC begins consultations with Kerala leaders in New Delhi to decide on Congress’ CM candidate
item.description: The high command, according to a party insider, is reportedly seeking bipartisan support of top leaders, including former KPCC presidents, to restore the period of organisational and alliance stability, which delivered the Congress the momentous win

item.title: Kash Patel clashes with Democratic senator over drinking allegations: ‘Unequivocally, categorically false’
item.description: Patel also sought to counterattack politically by referencing Van Hollen’s previous trip to El Salvador involving Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who had been detained there after an arrest in Maryland.

item.title: MGNREGS workers togo on strike on May 15
item.description: NREGA Sangharsh Morcha and All India Kisan Sabha have called for the strike against the government’s decision to discontinue the scheme.

item.title: Trump's Goal In China? Keep It Simple
item.description: Expectations are running high for the US-China summit in Beijing, but Trump’s main goal should be to not make things worse, the Editorial Board writes. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: 13 Killed In Israeli Strikes In Lebanon
item.description: Israeli strikes killed 13 people in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, including a soldier, a child and two rescue workers, the health ministry said.

item.title: ‘Bangla Pokkho’ founder Garga Chatterjee arrested for spreading EVM misinformation
item.description: Bangla Pokkho is a pro-Bengali advocacy organisation, and Mr. Chatterjee is often found campaigning for prioritising the Bengali language and Bengalis over other languages and communities in West Bengal

item.title: China’s Manufacturing Heartland Tested by Iran War Energy Shock
item.description: Economic activity in China’s most important manufacturing hub is heaping pressure on power supplies, sparking an energy stress test as the Iran war chokes shipments of fuel.

item.title: Trinamool’s land policy delayed West Bengal border fencing: MHA’s 2023 affidavit in Supreme Court
item.description: On December 11, 2023, the Ministry of Home Affairs informed the Supreme Court that the West Bengal government had not adopted the LARR Act 2013, which allows land acquisition for public purposes

item.title: CommScope Sued by Lenders for at Least $150 Million Over Alleged Breach
item.description: CommScope Holding Co. was sued by a group of lenders who allege the telecommunications provider breached a debt agreement by failing to pay a prescribed premium of at least $150 million following a key asset sale.

item.title: S&P 500 & NASDAQ Fall as Oil Jumps | Closing Bell
item.description: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Google thwarts hacker group’s AI-driven mass exploitation plan - Crypto Briefing
item.description: Google thwarts hacker group’s AI-driven mass exploitation plan Crypto Briefing

item.title: Tiger Woods' DUI case: Florida prosecutors to get golfer's prescription drug records
item.description: A Florida judge on Tuesday ruled that prosecutors can get access to Tiger Woods’ prescription drug records in his DUI case. Woods faces charges after a March crash in Jupiter involving alleged speeding, impairment signs, and refusal of a urine test.

item.title: What Trump and Xi Want From Their Summit
item.description: The leaders are expected to talk trade, Taiwan, and the Iran war.

item.title: Bond Bears Reload Fed Rate Hike Wagers on Stubborn Inflation
item.description: Traders have renewed bearish bets on US Treasuries, lifting expectations that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates as oil prices and inflation push higher.

item.title: Trump Says US Inflation Is 'Short Term'
item.description: President Donald Trump says inflation in the US is only "short term." He spoke before leaving for Beijing on Tuesday. US inflation accelerated in April due to rising gasoline and grocery costs, exceeding wage growth. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: World News Today Live Updates on May 13, 2026 : Cannes 2026 opens with Peter Jackson tribute, Jane Fonda’s remarks and James Franco return on Day 1
item.description: World News Today Live Updates on May 13, 2026: Stay informed on global events with our in-depth world news coverage, bringing you the latest developments across politics, economy, and culture. Discover insights into international affairs, breaking news, and the trends shaping our interconnected world, all in one place.

item.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Nasdaq end lower as inflation, Iran tensions weigh
item.description: US stocks closed lower as hotter-than-expected inflation and escalating US-Iran tensions weighed on sentiment. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq retreated from record highs, led by tech weakness, while Dow held steady. Rising oil prices and fears of prolonged inflation reduced hopes of Fed rate cuts and lifted rate-hike bets.

item.title: Baltimore ship crash: U.S. slaps criminal cases against Singapore, Chennai firms and Indian national
item.description: They have been charged with conspiracy, wilfully failing to immediately inform U.S. Coast Guard of a known hazardous condition, obstruction of an agency proceeding, and false statements

item.title: Lebanon says two paramedics among 13 killed in Israeli strikes
item.description: Lebanon's health ministry says the paramedics were carrying out a rescue mission in Nabatieh in response to an earlier attack that killed one person.

item.title: Introspect on reputational cost of aiding terror exporter Pakistan, India tells China
item.description: India's MEA urged China to consider the reputational damage of backing Pakistan's terrorist infrastructure, following reports of Chinese tech assistance during last year's Operation Sindoor. The ministry stated these reports corroborated existing knowledge, emphasizing that supporting such infrastructure impacts a nation's standing.

item.title: Breaking: Mumbai-based Sarvodaya Cooperative Bank licence cancelled by RBI - MSN
item.description: Breaking: Mumbai-based Sarvodaya Cooperative Bank licence cancelled by RBI MSN
